history.text: Norse settlement shaped the language, place names and island culture of the Faroe Islands.
The islands became tied to the Danish crown through the Norway-Denmark union.
Home rule gave the Faroe Islands broad self-government within the Kingdom of Denmark.

worthSeeing.text: A compact North Atlantic capital with Tinganes, harbour lanes and turf-roofed buildings.
worthSeeing.title: Tórshavn:

worthSeeing.text: Northern Isles base for fishing-harbour views, mountain roads and island day trips.
worthSeeing.title: Klaksvík:

worthSeeing.text: Ferries, tunnels and cliff roads make the islands feel much larger than the map suggests.
worthSeeing.title: Coastal routes:

planningQuestions.a: Tórshavn, Klaksvík and dramatic North Atlantic coastal routes.
